title: Text Inspection, Search, and Structured Transformation · Independent Operations Trial
summary: Extract a defensible incident summary from mixed logs without silently corrupting delimiters, encodings, ordering, or duplicate policy. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a tested text-processing pipeline with fixtures with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select cat, less, head, tail, wc, file, and od from file size, format, encoding, and inspection goal.
Use fixed strings, basic or extended regular expressions, anchors, classes, context, and exit status with explicit locale assumptions.
Separate find traversal, predicates, grouping, pruning, printing, and actions while protecting path boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Logs contain duplicate IDs, spaces, UTF-8 names, and severities high, low, and HIGH. Produce a stable 5-row summary with explicit locale, delimiter, regex, sort key, and duplicate policy.
